concept: max path deviation <= 0.5mm (geometric edge fidelity target)
implication: The pipeline treats a traced path as faithful only when it stays within 0.5mm of the source edge. A tracer that rounds or simplifies edges beyond this tolerance triggers human review. This is a hard geometric-fidelity bound on how coarsely edges may be approximated.

param_mapping.rationale: path_precision sets the decimal precision of emitted path coordinates; higher precision keeps traced edges closer to the source, directly serving the <=0.5mm deviation bound. To honor a tight geometric tolerance the search should bias path_precision toward the top of its ladder (7-8) rather than the coarse end (3-4). The 0.5mm figure is a downstream target, not a vtracer unit, so this couples the doctrine bound to path_precision as the fidelity lever (inferred coupling, directly-stated target).

hypothesis: Increasing path_precision toward 8 reduces traced-edge deviation from the source, raising edge_iou and helping meet the doctrine's <=0.5mm deviation gate, because coordinate precision is what bounds how far the vector edge can drift from the raster edge.
